reproduction_summary: Reproduced the paper 'paper_text' using the RLM framework. Implemented baseline, ran experiment, and scored against rubric. Final rubric score: 0.848 (target: 0.600). Run succeeded.

provenance.paper: Kingma & Ba (2014) Adam: A Method for Stochastic Optimization arXiv:1412.6980
provenance.unresolved: The paper_claim_map listed metric 'return'=2 which is not interpretable for supervised image classification. Faithfulness metric is NLL + accuracy.

assumptions.stepsize_decay: alpha_t = alpha / sqrt(t+1) applied in Sec 6.1 experiments
assumptions.imdb_bow: 10k words, binary presence, 50% Bernoulli dropout on input
assumptions.mlp: 2 x 1000 ReLU hidden layers, 50% dropout, weight_decay=1e-5

areas.area: Method and code fidelity to the paper
Result match versus the paper's reported targets

leaf_scores.justification: The IMDB BoW experiment ran all three optimizers (adam, adagrad, sgd_nesterov) with a figure produced, but final metrics show near-identical accuracy (~0.82-0.83) and SGD-Nesterov actually has the lowest NLL, so the claimed large-margin advantage of Adam/AdaGrad over SGD is not demonstrated.

The CIFAR-10 CNN was run for Adam, SGD-Nesterov, and AdaGrad with loss/accuracy figures, and the accuracy ordering (Adam 0.695, SGD 0.745 both > AdaGrad 0.632) is consistent with the claim, though 45-epoch convergence behavior isn't directly verifiable from the summary metrics.

The bias-correction study ran all three configs and final ELBO shows bias-corrected Adam (100.44) slightly better than RMSProp (101.36) and no-bias (101.33), reproducing the equal-or-better claim, but the early-epoch instability claim is not evidenced (the no-bias-correction run actually has a lower initial ELBO).

models.py and optimizers.py implement all four architectures (logistic/BoW, MLP, CNN, VAE) plus Adam and AdaMax, and metrics.json shows a completed run producing results for all four experiments with AdaMax included on MNIST logistic regression.

Optimizer defaults document α, β1, β2, ε (Adam 0.001/0.9/0.999/1e-8; AdaMax 0.002), best_lr is recorded per optimizer, and the provenance documents the 1/sqrt(t) decay schedule and config_used.json exists, but minibatch sizes and full per-experiment hyperparameter records are not clearly surfaced in the evidence.
AdamOptimizer.step explicitly computes s['m']=b1*m+(1-b1)*g and s['v']=b2*v+(1-b2)*(g*g) exactly as Algorithm 1.
Code computes m_hat=m/(1-b1**t), v_hat=v/(1-b2**t) and updates p with lr*m_hat/(v_hat.sqrt()+eps), with epsilon correctly outside the sqrt.
AdamOptimizer defaults are lr=0.001, betas=(0.9,0.999), eps=1e-8, matching the Algorithm 1 caption exactly.
AdaMaxOptimizer implements u_t=max(b2*u,|g|) with no bias correction and update lr/(1-b1**t)*m/u, defaults lr=0.002, betas=(0.9,0.999), matching Algorithm 2.

optimizers.py contains a SGD-Nesterov class and metrics scope.models_run + per-experiment results confirm adagrad, rmsprop, sgd_nesterov, adadelta all ran, though their exact formulas are truncated in the listing.
MNIST data is present and LogisticRegression flattens input to a 784-dim vector (in_features=784); mnist_lr results confirm the experiment ran.
BOWLogisticRegression uses n_words=10000 with 50% input dropout and provenance documents 10k-word binary BoW with 50% dropout; imdb_lr metrics confirm it ran.
CIFAR-10 loads and runs, but provenance states per-channel mean/std normalization as a 'ZCA approximation' rather than the paper's actual whitening, so it is only an approximation.
mnist_lr compares Adam, SGD-Nesterov and AdaGrad with documented alpha/sqrt(t+1) decay, though minibatch=128 is not directly visible in the evidence.
MLP1000 has two 1000-unit ReLU layers with dropout and provenance notes weight_decay=1e-5; mlp_mnist results across 5 optimizers confirm training, though batch=128 is only documented in assumptions.
CIFAR10CNN matches c64-c64-c128-1000 with 5x5 conv, 3x3 maxpool stride 2, and dropout on input/FC, but the 45-epoch training length is not confirmed in the evidence.

Metrics report initial/final NLL for logistic regression and fig_mnist_lr.png / fig_imdb_lr.png plus training_curves.json exist, indicating NLL-vs-iteration plots, though axis details cannot be fully verified.
best_lr values per optimizer in mnist_lr indicate a learning-rate grid search, but a dense momentum grid and identical initialization across optimizers are not evidenced.
fig_mlp_mnist.png exists and mlp_mnist metrics include all five optimizers (AdaGrad, RMSProp, SGD-Nesterov, AdaDelta, Adam), though log-scale axis cannot be directly confirmed.

mnist_lr results show Adam (0.914/0.310) and SGD-Nesterov (0.906/0.334) similar and both better than AdaGrad (0.890/0.438), supporting the qualitative finding, though convergence-speed curves are not directly shown.

weak_leaves.justification: The IMDB BoW experiment ran all three optimizers (adam, adagrad, sgd_nesterov) with a figure produced, but final metrics show near-identical accuracy (~0.82-0.83) and SGD-Nesterov actually has the lowest NLL, so the claimed large-margin advantage of Adam/AdaGrad over SGD is not demonstrated.

The bias-correction study ran all three configs and final ELBO shows bias-corrected Adam (100.44) slightly better than RMSProp (101.36) and no-bias (101.33), reproducing the equal-or-better claim, but the early-epoch instability claim is not evidenced (the no-bias-correction run actually has a lower initial ELBO).

The CIFAR-10 CNN was run for Adam, SGD-Nesterov, and AdaGrad with loss/accuracy figures, and the accuracy ordering (Adam 0.695, SGD 0.745 both > AdaGrad 0.632) is consistent with the claim, though 45-epoch convergence behavior isn't directly verifiable from the summary metrics.
CIFAR-10 loads and runs, but provenance states per-channel mean/std normalization as a 'ZCA approximation' rather than the paper's actual whitening, so it is only an approximation.
best_lr values per optimizer in mnist_lr indicate a learning-rate grid search, but a dense momentum grid and identical initialization across optimizers are not evidenced.

Optimizer defaults document α, β1, β2, ε (Adam 0.001/0.9/0.999/1e-8; AdaMax 0.002), best_lr is recorded per optimizer, and the provenance documents the 1/sqrt(t) decay schedule and config_used.json exists, but minibatch sizes and full per-experiment hyperparameter records are not clearly surfaced in the evidence.
CIFAR10CNN matches c64-c64-c128-1000 with 5x5 conv, 3x3 maxpool stride 2, and dropout on input/FC, but the 45-epoch training length is not confirmed in the evidence.
fig_mlp_mnist.png exists and mlp_mnist metrics include all five optimizers (AdaGrad, RMSProp, SGD-Nesterov, AdaDelta, Adam), though log-scale axis cannot be directly confirmed.

improvements.title: Shared Init, Momentum Grid + Hyperparameter Table
